title: Feeling a little freaked out
body:
I'm a 51 year old female with a long complicated medical history including lupus, kidney failure, and many other complications over the past 30 years or so. Because I have a kidney transplant I take medicine that suppresses my immune system and gives me a much higher chance of cancers and infections. I've had my transplant for 8 years and so far I've been okay but, the past 2 weeks I have been having a strange symptom on my right side. It feels as if it is the colon rather than a muscle, because it's deeper than the stomach muscles.  I called my GI doctor and he blew it off, even though I have an endoscopy and biopsy is coming up, they are for the upper GI rather than the lower. My stomach, or more like my GI tract, has been unusually gassy for the past couple months, and I can't figure out why but it seems to be worse. I seem to be having more frequent bowel movements also.  I wouldn't call what is happening a normal spasm, like what you get when you have to go to the bathroom if you have food poisoning or diarrhea or any of that kind of thing. It's really hard to explain, but it's in the right side about 2 inch from my belly button and probably a couple inches deep. I have this strange fluttering spasm that's sort of rolls around, and feels almost like something is doing a backflip inside my colon LOL. I don't know how to describe it, except if you have had a baby and felt the baby roll around in there, it sort of feels a little like that.  I'm hundred percent not able to get pregnant or been around a man and I don't have any tube, appendix or ovary,so I know it's not any of those causing symptoms over there. I don't know if something is growing and obstructing the colon, so that when things move around in there it gives me that weird fluttering strange spasm, or what. I have searched and searched online, but I see nothing about it, because there is no pain and there really is no blood to speak of, or any of the obvious signs.

 One thing that freaks me out is that I have always been really careful not to hold a cell phone near my body or hold it up next to my head. But because I am disabled and I spend a lot of time sitting or lying in my bed when I'm not doing something, I tend to play a certain word game on my phone quite a lot or look at Reddit. I realized about a week ago that I am holding the phone very close to the place in my abdomen where I'm beginning to have these strange feelings. They have recently discovered a lot more about cell phones being near the body and causing tumors.  That really freaks me out, because I just realized I've been spending endless hours with the phone right next to my abdomen. I don't rest the phone on my body but I hold it very close, in line with my face over my stomach, like a total idiot. I don't even know what I was thinking, but now I'm being more careful to hold it off to the side of my body away from my abdomen.

My question is, has anyone with cancer ever had such a strange symptom? It happens to me repetitively throughout the day, and I can feel it a lot more when I'm lying down, but I also feel it when I'm sitting or standing at times. I was feeling it in my sleep and begin to dream that I was grabbing at whatever it was and it was under my skin, and first it was like disk-shaped and I was trying to squeeze it and see what it was, and then it turned into a frog, and I could see the frogs face through my skin LOL. I was thinking of getting a blade and trying to cut open the area and pull the thing out, not in real time but that was in my dream. But then I was thinking in my dream, how can I do that, because I will get an infection, and I can't cut into my stomach. Dreams are weird. 

I don't see the doctor till Monday, but I don't really know how to get anyone to really figure it out, because it seems like they really don't take it very seriously, and they're trying to tell me it's just gas or it's just a muscle cramp or something like that, but it definitely isn't. It's always in the same place and it happens many times a day.  

I really wish my abdominal wall was transparent right now....
